target_title: fix: unset context on stale promises
target_body:
When a stale promise is rejected in `async_derived`, and the promise eventually resolves, `d.resolve` will be noop and `d.promise.then(handler, ...)` will never run. That in turns means any restored context (via `(await save(..))()`) will never be unset. We have to handle this case and unset the context to prevent errors such as false-positive state mutation errors.

There's a separate error about the derived running multiple times when it shouldn't, but I haven't gotten around to that yet.

source_title: fix: don't rerun async effects unnecessarily
source_body:
Since #16866, when an async effect runs multiple times, we rebase older batches and rerun those effects. This can have unintended consequences: In a case where an async effect only depends on a single source, and that single source was updated in a later batch, we know that we don't need to / should not rerun the older batch.

This PR makes it so: We collect all the sources of older batches that are not part of the current batch that just committed, and then only mark those async effects as dirty which depend on one of those other sources. Fixes the bug I noticed while working on #16935

### Before submitting the PR, please make sure you do the following

- [ ] It's really useful if your PR references an issue where it is discussed ahead of time. In many cases, features are absent for a reason. For large changes, please create an RFC: https://github.com/sveltejs/rfcs

target_title: (fix) Product content edit: only fetch Dropbox files when pending files exist
target_body:
# Problem

While taking a look at https://github.com/antiwork/gumroad/issues/2042, I discovered that the product content editor polls for Dropbox file status updates every 10 seconds unconditionally (so even when there are no Dropbox files being uploaded). 

Demo with an accelerated interval (1s vs. 10s):

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a7736eb5-8573-41ca-98e2-887e84d9dd26



This causes:
- Unnecessary API requests/server usage every 10 seconds for the lifetime of the component
- Full re-renders of the content tab every 10 seconds

# Solution
Add a conditional check that only runs the polling interval when there are actually Dropbox files in the `in_progress` state. 

# AI Disclosure
Used Claude Sonnet 4.5 to review the solution.

target_title: perf: replace O(n*m) array lookups with O(1) Map lookups in attribute routing
target_body:
## What does this PR do?

Optimizes attribute routing lookups by replacing O(n*m) array-based scans with O(1) Map-based lookups. This significantly improves performance for enterprise organizations with many attributes and options.

**Changes:**
- `_buildAttributeLookupMaps`: New helper function that creates Maps for option ID → attribute and option ID → option
- `_getAttributeFromAttributeOption` and `_getAttributeOptionFromAttributeOption`: Now use Map.get() instead of nested .find()/.some()/.reduce()
- `_buildAssignmentsForTeam`: Builds lookup maps once and passes them to helper functions
- `_prepareAssignmentData`: Builds lookup maps once at the start for O(1) option lookups in `tranformContains`

**Performance Impact:**
The optimization replaces O(n×m) linear scans (where n = number of attributes, m = options per attribute) with O(1) Map lookups. For enterprise scenarios with ~6,500 options across 14 attributes, benchmarks showed ~18x speedup. The improvement scales with data size.

target_title: perf(admin): optimize concurrency-queue-backfill endpoint
target_body:
## Summary
- **Pipeline Redis writes** for queued jobs using `redis.pipeline()` instead of sequential awaits (3N round trips → 1 batch)
- **Increase ZSCAN COUNT** from 20 to 1000 to reduce Redis round trips when scanning existing concurrency queues
- **Use `ZCOUNT` instead of `ZRANGEBYSCORE().length`** to count active jobs without transferring all member strings over the wire
- **Parallelize ACUC team lookups** for Crawl and Extract concurrency limits with `Promise.all`
- **Return detailed response** with per-team stats (backlogged, queued, started) so callers can see progress

## New response format
```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "teamsProcessed": 43,
  "totalJobsQueued": 1200,
  "totalJobsStarted": 86,
  "teams": [
    {
      "teamId": "abc-123",
      "backloggedJobs": 50,
      "alreadyQueued": 20,
      "queued": 28,
      "started": 2
    }
  ]
}
```

## Test plan
- [ ] Call endpoint with `?teamId=<id>` for a single team, verify response includes stats
- [ ] Call endpoint without `teamId`, verify it processes all backlogged teams and returns aggregate stats
- [ ] Verify jobs are correctly queued and started (same behavior as before, just faster)

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Optimized the admin concurrency-queue-backfill endpoint to scan and queue jobs faster and return per-team progress. Adopted main’s pushConcurrencyLimitedJobs and kept all other Redis optimizations.

- **Performance**
  - Use pushConcurrencyLimitedJobs
source_title: fix(api): skip precrawl team in concurrency queue backfill
source_body:
## Summary
- The precrawl team (`86100d9a-54ec-43ab-8f90-ba5852aede34`, owned by @mogery) has ~2.2M backlogged jobs
- When the `/admin/.../concurrency-queue-backfill` endpoint runs without a `teamId`, it iterates all teams and gets stuck trying to process 2.2M jobs for this team, timing out before finishing
- This blocks the endpoint from processing any teams that come after it alphabetically
- This PR filters out the precrawl team ID so the backfill endpoint can successfully process all other teams

## Test plan
- [ ] Call `POST /admin/.../concurrency-queue-backfill` without a `teamId` and verify it completes without timing out
- [ ] Verify passing `?teamId=86100d9a-54ec-43ab-8f90-ba5852aede34` explicitly is also filtered

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Skip the precrawl team (86100d9a-54ec-43ab-8f90-ba5852aede34) in the concurrency queue backfill to prevent timeouts and let the endpoint complete for other teams. The team is filtered out even when passed via ?teamId.

<sup>Written for commit c1d76a3f766f539baee5c2fb811e291a256ea36c. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

source_title: Fix command palette auto-close when focus was in inline chat
source_body:
When opening command palette (F1) while focus is in inline chat, the palette immediately closes. This happens because `InlineChatAffordance` unconditionally focuses the editor when the input widget hides, stealing focus from the newly opened command palette.

**Changes:**
- Remove unconditional `editor.focus()` from the autorun in `InlineChatAffordance` that watches for widget position changes
- Move focus logic into `InlineChatInputWidget._hide()`, guarded by `dom.isAncestorOfActiveElement(editorDomNode)` to only focus the editor when focus is still within the editor's DOM tree

This preserves the expected behavior of returning focus to the editor when dismissing inline chat normally, while allowing focus to move elsewhere (command palette, quick open, etc.) without interference.

target_title: feat(pdf): Implement PDF Detection, and Text Extraction
target_body:
<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
## Summary by cubic
Adds Rust-powered PDF detection/extraction via pdf-inspector with parser modes fast, auto, and ocr. Rust runs when PDF_RUST_EXTRACT_ENABLE is on and the PDF is eligible; fast mode skips OCR fallback and now returns a clear error if the PDF needs OCR. Otherwise we fall back to RunPod MU (≤19MB) then parse-pdf, with the per‑page time budget only on fallback.

- **New Features**
  - Parser mode option: "fast" | "auto" | "ocr" (ocr uses detect-only for metadata, then OCR).
  - Fast mode on scanned/image PDFs returns SCRAPE_PDF_OCR_REQUIRED with guidance to use auto/ocr.
  - Returns pdf_type, markdown, page_count, processing_time_ms, pages_needing_ocr, title, confidence, is_complex.
  - Eligibility: TextBased, confidence ≥0.95, non‑complex, non‑empty markdown; enforces maxPages using Rust page count/title.
  - Mode handling: PDF_RUST_EXTRACT_ENABLE gates Rust for fast/auto; fast disables OCR fallback; invalid modes and shorthand strings are rejected.
  - MU is called only when Rust isn’t used or yields no result; caches MU results when possible.

- **Refactors**
  - Split engines into modules (runpodMU, pdfParse, types); replaced lopdf with pdf-inspector process_pdf/detect_pdf.
  - Simplified env flag logic and imports; improved timing/eligibility logs.
  - Updated pdf-inspector to rev f37c911.

<sup>Written for commit a95ba90e73d04906d4923d485b7e786d6a5d393e. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>
source_title: fix(api): use TransportableError for branding validation errors
source_body:
Branding extraction throws plain `Error` when the user requests branding on a non-HTML resource (PDF, document) or when Chrome CDP isn't available. These are expected user-input validation errors, not application bugs. Adds a `BrandingNotSupportedError` (`TransportableError` subclass) so these get handled as known errors in the scrapeURL error cascade instead of falling through to `captureException`.

Fixes https://firecrawl.sentry.io/issues/API-AV
Fixes https://firecrawl.sentry.io/issues/API-N7

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Treat branding unsupported cases as known user validation errors instead of unhandled exceptions. Adds a TransportableError subclass (BrandingNotSupportedError) and handles it in scrapeURL.

- **Bug Fixes**
  - Throw BrandingNotSupportedError when branding is requested for PDFs, documents, or when Chrome CDP is unavailable.
  - Added SCRAPE_BRANDING_NOT_SUPPORTED and error-serde mapping for transport/deserialize.
  - Updated scrapeURL to catch and warn on BrandingNotSupportedError, reducing noisy error reporting.

target_title: fix(ui): prevent infinite spinner on factor-two without active 2FA session
target_body:
## Summary

Users navigating directly to `/sign-in#/factor-two` without an active sign-in requiring 2FA would see an infinite loading spinner. This adds a status check to redirect them back to sign-in start, matching the existing pattern in `SignInFactorOne`.

- Added `useEffect` to `SignInFactorTwo` that redirects when `signIn.status` is `null`, `needs_identifier`, or `needs_first_factor`
- Dependency array only includes `__internal_setActiveInProgress` (not `signIn.status`) to avoid flash during valid sign-in completion

## Why not `signIn.status !== 'needs_second_factor'`?

That condition catches `complete` status, causing a flash to the sign-in page before the final redirect. By explicitly checking for invalid statuses only, we avoid interfering with the normal sign-in completion flow.

## Test plan

- [x] Direct navigation to `/sign-in#/factor-two` without active sign-in → redirects to sign-in start
- [x] Valid MFA sign-in flow → completes without flash to sign-in page

source_title: fix(ui): redirect signed-in users forward from factor-two
source_body:
## Summary

Follow-up to #7774. Builds on the infinite spinner fix by improving the redirect behavior for multi-session apps.

When a signed-in user lands on factor-two without a pending 2FA session (e.g. the dashboard reloads after successful verification), the previous fix redirected back to sign-in start which isn't ideal since the user is already authenticated.

This changes the behavior so that:
- Signed-in users with no pending 2FA get redirected forward to `afterSignInUrl`
- Users who aren't signed in still get redirected back to sign-in start

This is experimental - we want to verify on the dashboard before deciding whether to keep it.

target_title: feat(redteam): add numTests config option for strategy test capping
target_body:
## Summary

- Add `numTests` configuration option to strategies that acts as an absolute cap on the number of tests generated
- Allows users to limit test output per-strategy independently of plugin counts (e.g., `base64` doesn't need 5 tests per plugin)
- Follows the pattern already established by the retry strategy

## Changes

1. **Schema**: Added `numTests: z.number().int().min(0).optional()` to `StrategyConfigSchema`
2. **Capping logic**: Post-execution capping in `applyStrategies()` with warning log for `numTests: 0`
3. **Progress bar**: Updated `getTestCount()` to reflect capped counts accurately
4. **Tests**: Added 18 new tests for schema validation and capping behavior

## Example Usage

```yaml
strategies:
  - basic
  - jailbreak           # uses default fan-out (n=5)
  - id: base64
    numTests: 2         # cap at 2 total, regardless of plugin count
  - id: leetspeak
    numTests: 0         # skip entirely (logs warning)
```

## Semantic Distinction

- `n` (existing): Multiplier - "generate n tests PER input" (e.g., jailbreak default n=5)
- `numTests` (new): Absolute cap - "generate AT MOST this many total"

## Test plan

- [x] Schema validation tests (accepts 0, positive; rejects negative, floats, strings, NaN, Infinity)
- [x] Capping behavior tests for basic, layer, fan-out strategies
- [x] Retry strategy unchanged (has different additive semantics)
- [x] TypeScript compiles cleanly
- [x] All 103 tests pass

source_title: fix(redteam): apply strategy numTests as pre-generation limit
source_body:
## Summary

Follow-up fix to #7030 - changes the strategy `numTests` configuration to act as a **pre-generation limit** instead of a post-generation cap.

**Problem with #7030:**
The initial implementation applied `numTests` as a post-generation cap, meaning ALL applicable test cases were processed by the strategy, then the results were sliced to `numTests`. This wasted computation for expensive strategies (especially those making network calls).

**This PR fixes it:**
- Test cases are now sliced to `numTests` BEFORE passing to strategy
- Strategy only processes the needed tests
- Post-cap retained as safety net for 1:N fan-out strategies

## Changes

- Add early exit for `numTests=0` (skips strategy entirely with `continue`)
- Add pre-limit logic that slices `applicableTestCases` BEFORE calling `strategyAction`
- Update post-cap logging to WARN level (safety net for 1:N fan-out strategies)
- Add tests verifying strategy receives pre-limited input

## Verification

```
[Strategy] base64: Pre-limiting 5 tests to numTests=2
[Strategy] rot13: numTests=0 configured, skipping strategy
```

## Test plan

- [x] Unit test: strategy receives exactly numTests test cases when numTests < applicable
- [x] Unit test: strategy not called when numTests=0 (early exit)
- [x] Unit test: post-cap safety net works for 1:N fan-out strategies
- [x] All existing "strategy numTests capping integration" tests pass
- [x] TypeScript compiles cleanly
- [x] Lint passes

target_title: Optimize sales tax summary report with SQL GROUP BY
target_body:
## Problem

The global sales tax summary job (#3710) loads ~293k purchases one by one. Both the Jan and Feb 2026 runs timed out after 73+ minutes — no reports were generated or emailed.

## Approach

Move the aggregation into SQL. A single `GROUP BY` query reduces 293k rows to ~58k grouped results, then Ruby handles country normalization and state resolution on the smaller set. The ~12 US purchases with bad zip codes still fall back to GeoIp lookup individually.

Grouping uses byte-exact keys to avoid MySQL collation silently merging values like `"USA"` and `"usa"`. Country/state normalization logic is – just operates on query results instead of AR objects.

## Production validation (read replica, Jan 2026)

| Metric | Before | After |
|--------|--------|-------|
| Runtime | 73+ min (timed out) | **~30s** |
| Rows iterated in Ruby | 293,009 | 57,851 |

---

This PR was implemented with AI assistance using Claude Code for code generation. All code was self-reviewed.
source_title: Fix global sales tax summary report timeout on primary DB
source_body:
Issue #3669
Issue antiwork/gumroad-private/issues/164

## What

Split the refund `LEFT JOIN` out of the main aggregation query in `CreateGlobalSalesTaxSummaryReportJob`. Refunds are now pre-fetched in a separate lightweight query and merged in Ruby, following the same pattern as `resolve_geoip_fallback_purchases`.

## Why

The `LEFT JOIN` on the refunds table exceeds the 1-hour `max_execution_time` on the primary database. This was missed in #3825 because the query was validated on the read replica (~188s), but Sidekiq workers connect to the primary (`USE_DB_WORKER_REPLICAS` is nil) where write load makes the JOIN too slow.

**Verified on primary** (Jan 2026, 293K purchases):

| Approach | Primary | Replica |
|---|---|---|
| GROUP BY **with** refund LEFT JOIN (old) | >1h (timeout) | 188.0s |
| Split: prefetch + GROUP BY + Ruby merge (new) | **182.3s** | 38.6s |

**Correctness** — 0 mismatches comparing old vs new approach:

| Month | Groups compared | Mismatches |
|---|---|---|
| Jan 2026 | 57,853 | 0 |
| Feb 2026 | 55,395 | 0 |

---

This PR was implemented with AI assistance using Claude Code for code generation. All code was self-reviewed.

## What does this PR do?

Adds a `displayLocations` property to the `OptInFeatureConfig` interface to control where opt-in features are displayed (settings page, banner, or both), and applies this filtering at the callers' side.

source_title: fix(mcp): fix stdio server lifecycle and shutdown
source_body:
## Summary

This PR fixes the MCP stdio server to properly stay alive and handle shutdown, superseding #7001 with additional improvements:

- **Root cause fix**: Return a blocking Promise from `startStdioMcpServer()` that only resolves on shutdown, preventing `main.ts` from calling `shutdownGracefully()` prematurely
- **HTTP server cleanup**: Add `mcpServer.close()` call to HTTP server shutdown for consistent cleanup
- **Double-shutdown prevention**: Add `isShuttingDown` guard flag to prevent race conditions
- **Client disconnect handling**: Add `process.stdin.once('end', shutdown)` handler for graceful shutdown when client disconnects
- **Remove dead code**: Remove unused `transport.onclose` assignment (replaced by stdin handler)
- **Telemetry consistency**: Move telemetry outside the Promise in stdio server

- [x] Manual verification: stdio server stays alive and responds to requests
- [x] Manual verification: SIGINT triggers graceful shutdown

target_body: Issue: #2984 # Description ## Problem After the Tailwind migration, buttons using color variants (like `color="primary"`) were appearing invisible or with incorrect backgrounds in certain contexts. This was particularly noticeable in: - **ImageUploader** (thumbnail/logo upload button) - invisible against gray placeholder background - **Rich text editor** buttons in product descriptions The root cause was `bg-transparent` in the base `buttonVariants` class string. This conflicted with color variant classes like `bg-primary`, and due to CSS specificity/ordering, the transparent background was winning over the intended colored background. ## Solution Instead of using `!important` modifiers (bad practice), implemented a clean fix: 1. **Removed `bg-transparent` from base styles** ([Button.tsx] 2. **Added a new `default` color variant** with `bg-transparent` 3. **Set `color: "default"` in `defaultVariants`** so buttons without a color prop still get transparent background --- # Before/After # Before --- # After

target_title: Add workflow to close stale issues
target_body:
Addresses https://github.com/antiwork/gumroad/issues/3789#issuecomment-3995544199

## What

Adds a `close-stale-issues.yml` GitHub Actions workflow that automatically warns and closes inactive issues – the same approach used by `close-stale-prs.yml` (added in #3751), adapted for issues.

The workflow runs every 6 hours (and on manual dispatch). It checks all open issues for the last meaningful activity (non-bot comments), marks issues inactive for 7 days with a `stale` label and warning comment, then closes them after 8 days of inactivity. If activity resumes, the stale label is automatically removed.

## Why

The stale issue workflow was reported as not working, but only the stale PRs workflow was in place. Stale issues are now managed with the same policy as stale PRs.

---

This PR was implemented with AI assistance using Claude Opus 4.6.
source_title: Fix stale workflow grace period and add PR activity tracking
source_body:
## What

Fixes two bugs in the stale issues/PRs workflows introduced in #3881:

1. **Grace period not respected**: The close check used `staleDays >= 8` (days since last activity), not time since the stale label was applied. On the first run, issues inactive for 8+ days were labeled stale and then closed ~6 hours later on the next cron run — despite the warning promising 24 hours. Now the close condition checks that 24 hours have elapsed since the stale label was actually added.

2. **Linked PRs ignored**: The issues workflow only counted non-bot comments as activity. Publishing a PR that references an issue didn't reset the stale clock, so issues could be marked stale while actively being worked on. Now `cross-referenced` events from open PRs count as activity on the issue.

Both fixes also apply the same grace period correction to the stale PRs workflow.

## Why

After #3881 shipped, issues like #3810 were warned and closed within hours of each other because they had been inactive long before the workflow existed. The "24 hours" promise in the stale warning should be a real guarantee. And developers working on a fix via PR shouldn't have the issue closed underneath them.

---

This PR was implemented with AI assistance using Claude Opus 4.6.

- The `useEffect` that syncs `selectedPlugins` to config had a dependency cycle: effect runs → `updatePlugins()` modifies `config.plugins` → effect runs again → infinite loop

## Root Cause

The `updatePlugins()` function has merge logic that can produce output different from input (spreading existing plugin properties). When the effect compares the newly constructed plugins with current state, they may differ even though they're semantically equivalent, causing unnecessary updates and infinite loops.

## Fix

Added normalized comparison before calling `updatePlugins()`:
- Normalizes plugins to only compare meaningful properties (`id` and `config`)
- Only calls `updatePlugins()` when there's an actual semantic change
- Properly keeps `config.plugins` in the dependency array (correct React pattern)

This addresses the root cause rather than working around it with `getState()`.

## Test plan

- [x] Unit tests pass (`npm test -- --run src/pages/redteam/setup/components/Plugins.test.tsx`)
- [x] TypeScript compilation passes
- [x] Linting passes
- [ ] Manual test: Select EU AI Act preset on Plugins page - should not crash
source_title: fix(redteam): fix infinite loop in updatePlugins by comparing output vs state
source_body:
## Summary

This PR fixes the root cause of the infinite loop bug in the Plugins page by moving the fix to `updatePlugins` in `useRedTeamConfig.ts`.

### The Bug

The previous fix (#6626) added a `normalizePlugin` workaround in `Plugins.tsx`, but the actual root cause was in `updatePlugins`:

1. `updatePlugins` compared **INPUT** vs current state **before** running merge logic
2. The merge logic preserves extra properties (like `numTests`) from existing plugins
3. This caused the merged **OUTPUT** to differ from the **INPUT**
4. Result: effect calls `updatePlugins` → state changes → effect runs again → infinite loop

### The Fix

Compare **OUTPUT** (after merge) vs current state instead of **INPUT** vs state. This correctly detects when no actual change would occur.

**Benefits:**
- Fixes the root cause in one place
- Benefits all callers of `updatePlugins`
- Removes the `normalizePlugin` workaround from `Plugins.tsx`
- More testable (can unit test the hook directly)

## Test plan

- [x] Added 7 unit tests for `updatePlugins` covering:
  - Basic update functionality
  - No update when merged output equals current state
  - Preservation of extra properties during merge
  - String plugins
  - Mixed string/object plugins
  - Config merging
  - Infinite loop prevention scenario
- [x] Updated test comments in `Plugins.test.tsx`
- [x] All 2146 tests pass
- [x] TypeScript compilation passes
- [x] Lint passes
